reasoning: TIME Person of the Year repeat winners are historically almost exclusively national leaders or presidents (e.g. Obama, Bush, Clinton, Gorbachev, Stalin). Non-politicians like Jeff Bezos, Mark Zuckerberg, or Bill Gates have never won twice. Elon Musk won in 2021. A repeat win for him in 2025 (only 4 years later) would be unprecedented for a business figure, even despite his increased political involvement. Without access to prediction market data, the historical base rate of repeat winners dictates a very low probability.
